返回本批次

脑腐工业园区

The Brainrot Industrial Complex

jshamsul.com 作者 jibone 分数 29 评论 15 2026/04/12 12:31
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 2026-04-12> 你没有分心。你正在被处理。愿你平安，数字漫游者。
  2. 也许没有比网络俚语更能描述现代社会状况的术语了：“脑残”。年轻的数字原住民经常用这个短语来形容那种熟悉的状态，即大脑感到迟钝、过度刺激、无法长时间保持一个单一的想法。 “这个模因让我脑残”这个词经常被用来幽默地说，但笑话的背后却隐藏着一些真实的东西。有一些潜在的信号，因为缺乏更好的术语，大脑（一个人的意识）正在腐烂。有趣的是，这个术语既不是来自批评家，也不是来自学者。 “脑腐病”这个词来自于沉浸在其中的一代人。 A kind of self-aware diagnosis.他们感觉有些不对劲，即使他们没有完全阐明原因。 Here is how I would define “brainrot”:
  3. 脑腐病是指由于持续接触高刺激、低物质的数字输入而导致一个人的思考、专注和反思能力逐渐受到侵蚀。
  4. 这里的“工业综合体”一词源自德怀特·D·艾森豪威尔所推广的短语“军工综合体”。简单来说，它描述了政府、军队和私营工业之间的紧密关系，各方都从武器的持续生产和战争中受益。它不仅仅是一个生产武器的行业。这是一个完整的系统，旨在维持对它们的需求。今天我们正在目睹类似的事情。 “脑腐工业综合体”是一个旨在劫持你的注意力的整个产业，让你的大脑奖励中心尽可能廉价、大量地产生多巴胺，以实现利润最大化。该系统提供了这种“廉价的刺激”，作为对无聊、焦躁的补救措施，也许还可以缓解我们难以说出的更深层次的东西。现代互联网不再是为了向你提供信息而设计的，它现在的设计是为了消除你清晰思考的能力。 “脑腐”现象不仅是注意力分散，而且是衰退。曾经有一段时间，“分心”这个词的含义比今天要沉重得多。在古英语中，“分心”并不意味着暂时失去注意力，它过去的意思是“精神错乱”，思想四分五裂，无法集中注意力。这个词本身来自拉丁语distrahere，意思是分开，向不同的方向撕裂。今天，当我们分心时，我们会被什么吸引呢？想想这个。曾经的无序状况，现在已成为常态。曾经被描述为“思维被撕裂”的现象现在已成为现代互联网用户的默认状态。分心并不是一场新的危机。人类一直很容易受到影响。罗马人担心竞技场的壮观景象，他们哀叹游戏和八卦带来的道德败坏。维多利亚时代的人对那些抢走了更严肃的追求的注意力的小说感到恐慌。今天的不同之处在于规模、强度和意图。头脑腐烂的工业综合体并没有创造出新的弱点，而是将一直存在的弱点武器化。我们能做些什么呢？我们还无法拆除整个系统。它需要政策改革、平台监管和大量的积极行动。我并不是说我们不应该这样做，现在我只是想给你留下一些针对个人的实际补救措施。意识是第一步。我们无法拆除这个系统，但我们可以有意识地驾驭它。开始注意对你注意力的吸引，在滚动之前暂停。观察触发因素：无聊、焦虑、存在之痒。这些都是脑腐工业综合体所利用的。采取一些小步骤来重新吸引你的注意力。最重要的是更加有意识地消费数字媒体。不要让算法决定你应该沉迷于什么。有意识地选择进入你脑海的内容。要明白你并不无聊，你正在被处理。并不是因为你缺乏纪律而容易分心，而是因为你处于为脑腐而优化的机器中。对于那些建造者来说，要明白这一点：我们所居住的系统并不是不可避免的。它们是被设计的。提要、循环和无尽的滚动，这些都是选择，而不是自然法则。可以以不同的方式构建。设计尊重注意力的工具，而不是利用注意力。创建为用户服务而不是处理用户的协议。不同的互联网并不是幻想。这是一个意图问题。保持故障，保持人性。 Jibone.Canonical 存档 / 发布在 Substack 上 / 通过 Paragraph 镜像到链上 / 跨 Nostr 中继

Original Summary

  1. 2026-04-12> You Are Not Distracted. You Are Being Processed.Peace be upon you, fellow digital wanderer.
  2. There is perhaps no better term to describe the condition of modern society than the internet slang: “brainrot.” A phrase often used jokingly by the younger digital native generation to describe that familiar state where the mind feels dulled, overstimulated, unable to hold a single thought for long. “This meme gave me brainrot” The term is often said in humour, but beneath the joke lies something real. There are underlying signals of, for the lack of better term, rotting of the brain, one’s conscious mind. Interestingly, the term did not emerge from critics nor did it come from academics. The term “brainrot” came from the very generation that is immersed in it. A kind of self-aware diagnosis. They feel something is off, even if they don’t fully articulate why. Here is how I would define “brainrot”:
  3. Brainrot is the gradual erosion of one’s ability to think, focus, and reflect, caused by continuous exposure to high-stimulation, low-substance digital input.
  4. The term “Industrial Complex” here came from the phrase popularised by Dwight D. Eisenhower, “The Military-Industrial Complex”. In simple terms, it describes the tight relationship between government, military, and private industry, where each benefits from the continued production of weapons and wars. It is not merely an industry that produces weapons. It is a complete system designed to sustain the need for them. And we are witnessing today something similar. The “brainrot industrial complex” is a whole industry designed to hijack your attention, by making your brain’s reward centers produce dopamine as cheaply and abundantly as possible to maximise profits. The system offers this “cheap thrills” as a remedy for boredom, for restlessness, and perhaps for something deeper we struggle to name. The modern internet is no longer designed to inform you, it is now designed to dissolve your ability to think clearly. The “brainrot” phenomena is not just distraction, but decay. There was a time when the word “distraction” carried a far heavier meaning than it does today. In older English, to be “distracted” did not mean to lose focus momentarily, it used to mean “to be mentally disturbed”, a mind pulled apart, unable to hold itself together. The term itself comes from the Latin distrahere, which means to draw apart, to tear in different directions. Today, when we get distracted, what are we drawn apart from? Think about this. What was once a condition of disorder, has now become a condition of normalcy. What was once described as the mind being “pulled apart”, is now the default state of the modern internet user. Distraction is not a new crisis. Humans have always been susceptible. The Romans worried about the spectacle of the Coliseum, they lamented moral decay from games and gossip. The Victorians panicked over novels that stole attention from more serious pursuits. What is different today is the scale, intensity, and intent. The brainrot industrial complex is not creating a new weakness, it is weaponising one that has always existed. What can we do about it? We are not yet in a position to dismantle the whole system. It requires policy reform, platform regulations, and a lot of activism. I’m not saying that we should not do that, for now, I just want to leave you with practical remedies for the individual self. Awareness is the first step. We cannot dismantle the system, but we can navigate it with intention. Start noticing the pulls on your attention, pause before you scroll. Observe the triggers: boredom, anxiety, existential itch. These are what the brainrot industrial complex exploits. Take small steps to reclaim your attention. Most important is to be more intentional in your digital media consumption. Do not let the algorithm decide what you should indulge. Choose consciously what enters your mind. Understand that you are not bored, you are being processed. It is not that you are lacking discipline that makes you easily distracted, it is because you are in the machine optimised for brainrot. And for those who build, understand this: the systems we inhabit are not inevitable. They are designed. The feeds, the loops, and the endless scroll, these are choices, not laws of nature. It is possible to build differently. To design tools that respect attention, rather than exploit it. To create protocols that serve users, rather than process them. A different internet is not a fantasy. It is a matter of intention. Stay glitched, stay human. Jibone.Canonical archive / Published on Substack / Mirrored on-chain via Paragraph / Relayed across Nostr

评论（默认中文）

chromacity 2026/04/12 13:29
和 当然 这是一篇人工智能生成的文章。
And of course it's an AI-generated article.
在 HN 中查看
dimator 2026/04/12 13:58
是吗？？那么我需要更新我的校准。是什么给你提示的？
Is it?? I need to update my calibration then. What tipped you off?
在 HN 中查看
szopa 2026/04/12 13:37
我开始觉得关于人工智能生成的文章的评论是超低价值和超低努力的。谁在乎？很快你要处理的大部分文本都将由人工智能生成。但人工智能生成的内容仍然有好有坏——开始根据其优点来判断它。
I’m starting to feel that comments about an article being AI-generated are super low value AND super low effort. Who cares? Soon most of the text you’ll be dealing with is gonna be AI generated. But there’s still good and bad AI generated content — start judging it by its merits.
在 HN 中查看
gwern 2026/04/12 13:50
查看空白和段落，意识到这是一个法学硕士，将其插入 Pangram 中查看它获得 100%（毫不奇怪），然后单击关闭；要容易得多；与其带着傻瓜般的诚意去阅读它，然后意识到它从来没有说过任何具体的、有意义的或不可预测的东西，而只包含像罐头词源或陈词滥调的引文这样的垃圾。
It's a lot easier to look at the whitespace and paragraphs, realize it's a LLM, plug it into Pangram to see that it gets 100% (unsurprisingly), and click to close; than it is to read it with a sucker's good faith and realize that it never says anything concrete or meaningful or unpredictable and contains only junk like canned etymologies or cliche quotes.
在 HN 中查看
allthetime 2026/04/12 13:45
“很快你要处理的大部分文本都将由人工智能生成。”

不，谢谢。我自己和许多其他人将继续寻找人类作家写下的真实想法。

你轻率地处理人际交往的心态和意愿令我深感担忧。

您不应该花时间来决定您经常使用的机器生成的数据是否具有特定的质量。

“Soon most of the text you’ll be dealing with is gonna be AI generated.”

No thanks. Myself and many others will continue to seek out real thoughts written by human writers.

Your mindset and willingness to flippantly dispose of human communication is deeply concerning to me.

Your days should not be spent deciding if the machine generated data you’re constantly consuming is of a specific calibre.

在 HN 中查看
lacy_tinpot 2026/04/12 13:52
看到“智力和思维使人类成为人类的原因”这一错误观念实时开始崩溃是很有趣的。这是人类身份的结构支柱之一，是由一些哲学家发明的，他们很久以前就迷失在自己的宏大之中，以至于我们都把它误认为是福音。这个想法一开始就是错误的，但我想我们很多人都忘记了这一点。因此，观察“作为人类”的发明部分如何被侵蚀，或者更确切地说正在经历一场革命，将会很有趣。

现实是，人类独立于这个想法。

思想和想法当然将继续属于人类的领域，但作为策展人/将我们的智力创造力扩展到单纯的写作技巧之外。因此，即使是人类是有思想的生物这一故事，在可预见的未来仍将继续存在。

It's fun to see the false idea that intelligence and thinking are what make humans human begin to collapse in real time. It's one of those structural pillars of human identity invented by some philosophers too lost in their own grandiosity from quite some time ago that we've all mistaken it for gospel. That notion was false to begin with, but I think a lot of us forgot that. So it'll be interesting to watch how that invented part of "being human" is eroded away, or rather is going through a revolution.

The reality is that being human stands independent of that idea.

Thoughts and ideas of course will continue to be the domain of humans, but as curators/extending our intellectual creativity beyond just the mere craft of writing. So even that story, that humans are thinking beings, will continue on for the foreseeable future.

operatingthetan 2026/04/12 13:57

一旦法学硕士开始思考，请告诉我们！

>It's fun to see the false idea that intelligence and thinking are what makes humans human begin to collapse in real time.

As soon as LLMs start thinking please let us know!

Cider9986 2026/04/12 13:44
如果人工智能生成不是对不良内容进行分类的可靠同义词，我会考虑这一点。

编辑：实际上我不会。我不想与死气沉沉的互联网互动。人工智能编辑或咨询，这是公平的。已生成——不用了，谢谢。

If AI-generated wasn't a reliable synonym for classifying bad content, I'd consider that.

edit: actually I wouldn't. I do not want to interact with an internet that is dead. AI-edited or consulted, that's fair. Generated—no thanks.

savolai 2026/04/12 14:08
就本文而言，这种呼吁似乎也毫无道理。

来自“关于”页面：“当我不编写代码时，我会写散文，或长或短，从个人科技轶事到关于技术如何塑造文化、社会和个人自我的哲学思考。”

In the case of this article, the calling out also seems to be without merit.

From the about page: ” When I’m not writing code, I write prose, short and long, ranging from personal anecdotes in tech to philosophical musings on how technology shapes culture, society, and the individual self.”

在 HN 中查看
Lammy 2026/04/12 13:45
推论：我们应该共同停止将 WWW 客户端称为“浏览器”，因为这个比喻不再合适。他们是网络掠食者： https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Browsing_%28herbivory%29
Corollary: we should collectively stop referring to WWW clients as “browsers” because the metaphor no longer fits. They are Web Grazers: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Browsing_%28herbivory%29
在 HN 中查看
egeozcan 2026/04/12 14:00
因此，人们认为现代互联网的功能就像一个“脑残工业综合体”（文章标题），故意设计来劫持我们的注意力并降低我们清晰思考的能力以获取利润……我的反驳是，现在一切不都是这样吗？互联网恰好是主要的沟通渠道。即使是过去十年左右我参加的本地面对面会议也充满了干扰、寻求关注和歪曲事实的情况。

是的，人们应该做出明确的努力来重新集中注意力，但也许不能直接使用数字工具？ “从物质世界开始”是我的拙见。

我坚信数字世界只是一切事物的乘数，包括我们的缺陷。所以我们应该从源头开始。

So it's agrued that the modern internet functions as a "brainrot industrial complex" (title of the article), deliberately designed to hijack our attention and degrade our ability to think clearly for profit... My counter-point is, isn't everything so these days? Internet just happens to be the main communication channel. Even the local, in-person meetings I've had in the last 10 years or so, are full of distractions, attention-seeking and misrepresentation.

Yes people should make an explicit effort to reclaim their focus, but maybe not directly with digital tools? "Start in the physical world" would be my humble advice.

I strongly believe the digital world is just a multiplier for everything, including our defects. So we should just start at the source.

在 HN 中查看
savolai 2026/04/12 14:05
这命名了一些有价值的东西并以原始的方式构建它。它表达了对年轻一代的关爱，鼓励努力与年轻人见面。它提供了有关如何练习注意力的实用建议。在当今时代，这是非常有价值的。

我看到它被实时标记，请唤醒 HN。

This names something valuable and frames it in an original way. It expressed caring for younger generations and encourages effort to meet the young with presence. It gives practical advice on how to practice attention. In present time this is enormously valuable.

I see it being flagged in realtime, please wake up HN.

在 HN 中查看
kruffalon 2026/04/12 13:54
这篇文章是脑残。

这是一个愤怒的诱饵，没有明确的想法来应对实际步骤 脑腐工业综合体。

它指出我们不能或什至不应该费心思考该怎么办，相反，它提供了超级通用、无益的自助指南，这几乎是不可能做到的，因为我们必须与现实作斗争。 脑腐工业园区 每个毫秒都成功，而他们只需要每小时（或更短）赢一次就可以让我们忙碌。

我完全赞成描述问题，甚至不试图寻找解决方案。

但这更糟糕：这假装提供了一个解决方案，所以我们得到了感觉良好的刺激，但实际上没有完成任何事情。

真正的脑残废话。

[编辑：] 拼写和格式

This article is brainrot.

It is ragebait with no clear idea on actual steps to take against the brainrot industrial complex.

It states that we can't or shouldn't even bother thinking about what to do about it instead it offers super generic unhelpful self-help guideline that is almost impossible to do since we must fight the brainrot industrial complex every ms to make it while they just have to win once an hour (or less) to keep us occupied.

I'm all for describing problems without even trying to find solutions.

But this is worse: this pretends to offer a solution so we get the kick of feeling good without actually accomplishing anything.

True brainrot crap article.

[Edit:] Spelling and formatting

在 HN 中查看
